wikitext_corpus: datasets/wiki.test.raw (Salesforce/wikitext wikitext-2-raw-v1 test split, 4358 rows, 241K words, 1.2 MB)

power_config.note: captured during decode smoke; active samples filtered to GPU > 1W to exclude idle start/end

variants.label: Gemma 4 26B-A4B fp16 slot-bank (16 slots, topk=8, prefetch-temporal)
Gemma 4 26B-A4B fp16 slot-bank (16 slots, topk=8, NO prefetch-temporal)
variants.purpose: numerics isolation: does disabling prefetch eliminate the 32% PPL delta vs stock?

findings: PPL is very high across all three Gemma-4-IT variants on wikitext-2-raw-v1 (range 11896 to 57163). This is the expected 'IT model on raw continuation' signal: the models are instruction-tuned, not base language models, so they score raw-text continuation poorly. Google does not release base weights for Gemma 4. Relative comparisons across variants are still meaningful.

Slot-bank PPL (38957) differs from stock PPL (57163) by ~32% on the same 26B-A4B GGUF — the Flash-MoE slot-bank path is NOT bit-identical to the stock MoE kernel on Gemma 4. Chunk 1 matches within <1% (5997 stock vs 6030 slot-bank), but subsequent chunks diverge, likely from --moe-prefetch-temporal biasing subsequent routing. Slot-bank is actually LOWER PPL (better) here, which is surprising — worth investigating whether stock mode is numerically off vs slot-bank's routing is closer to the intended Gemma 4 behavior.

Decode throughput confirms prior measurements: 31B Q8_0 = 14.6 tok/s, 26B-A4B stock fp16 = 50.3 tok/s, 26B-A4B slot-bank fp16 = 8.3 tok/s (6x slowdown from stock, consistent with 95%-IO-bound slot-bank path).
Slot-bank hit rate was 60.5% at ctx=2048, slightly above the 57.9% measured at ctx=4096 in T-0612 Session 24 — smaller context reduces expert working-set pressure.
Prefill perplexity run was fast on 26B-A4B stock (1606 tok/s prefill) because MoE 4B-active routing is lightweight for the prefill pass compared to dense 31B (269 tok/s).

slot_bank_numerics_investigation.question: Is slot-bank bit-identical to stock on Gemma 4 26B-A4B?
slot_bank_numerics_investigation.answer: No. Prefetch accounts for ~25% of the divergence; the remaining ~15% is in the slot-bank kernel itself or Metal shader implementation.

chunk_1_surprise.note: Stock and slot-bank+prefetch agree on chunk 1 to within 0.5%. Disabling prefetch drops chunk 1 to 3522.62 — half of stock. Suggests --no-moe-prefetch-temporal alters initial slot state or cold-start expert selection beyond just routing bias.

slot_bank_numerics_investigation.c7_decision: Pick slot-bank +prefetch as the canonical C7 config (performance-optimal at 7.17 tok/s, 57.9% hit rate). Document PPL divergence from stock (~32%) as an open upstream question for the anemll-flash-llama.cpp Gemma 4 MoE kernel. Do not claim slot-bank is bit-equivalent to stock in the final writeup.
hypotheses_to_investigate: (a) Stock mode's 128-expert MoE kernel has a numerical issue on Gemma 4 that slot-bank's repacked sidecar path happens to avoid (stock is WRONG, slot-bank is CORRECT).
(b) Slot-bank uses approximate top-k from cached experts when misses are pending, even though telemetry says refs ~= theoretical max.
(c) The Metal shader for the slot-bank matmul has different numerical behavior (fusion, accumulation order, precision) vs the stock MoE matmul.
slot_bank_numerics_investigation.mac_vs_pc_cross_harness_note: PC harness (tqp-v0.1.0 llama.cpp, Salesforce-source wikitext via ggml-org/ci mirror) reports stable per-chunk PPL 1700-2800 on 31B Config-I, cumulative = 2595. Mac harness reports climbing per-chunk PPL ending at 11896 on 31B Q8_0. Cross-node sanity run with Mac's committed wiki.test.raw queued for PC side to isolate harness/binary vs corpus as the 4.6x gap explanation.

mac_vs_pc_harness_investigation.question: On the same Gemma 4 31B IT model, why does PC report PPL=2595 while Mac reports PPL=11896?
mac_vs_pc_harness_investigation.resolution: The 4.6x gap is 4x harness/binary + 1.15x corpus. NOT corpus-dominant.

evidence.run: PC original, PC local mirror (ggml-org/ci, 1,305,088 bytes)
PC cross-corpus, Mac committed file (bbf94c..., 1,287,656 bytes after LF normalization)
Mac, Mac committed file (same bytes as above)

mac_vs_pc_harness_investigation.decision_required_for_C0: Pick one canonical llama-perplexity harness for the C0 ablation row. Options: (A) re-run Mac dense 31B on the tqp-v0.1.0 base so numbers compare directly with PC Config-I; (B) re-run PC Config-I via the anemll-flash-llama.cpp build so numbers compare with Mac MoE path; (C) report both harnesses side by side with a methodology footnote and treat PPL as a per-node correctness gate rather than a cross-node comparable.
mac_vs_pc_harness_investigation.windows_checkout_gotcha: Committed wiki.test.raw was silently converted LF -> CRLF on Windows git checkout (size 1,287,656 -> 1,290,547), changing SHA. PC user normalized to LF in /tmp/ before re-running. Added '.gitattributes' rule 'datasets/*.raw -text' so future Windows checkouts preserve LF bytes.
